Title: Malaysia's digital push must uplift all, not just urban groups
Article:
BUKIT MERTAJAM: Malaysia's digital transformation drive risks failure if it does not uplift the majority of the rakyat, as technological progress must go hand in hand with equity, values and access, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said.
He said while the push towards a digital economy — including artificial intelligence (AI), data centres and advanced technologies — is critical for growth, jobs and competitiveness, its success must ultimately be measured by how widely benefits are shared.
"Once we pursue this agenda to achieve digital transformation, it is certainly a failure if we are not able to ensure that the vast majority of our people benefit from this," he said in his speech at the launch of Kubang Semang Nadi in Yayasan Aman here.
Anwar said Malaysia is in a strong position to attract global investments from countries such as the United States, China, India and Germany, but cautioned that this advantage must not translate into gains for only urban or well-connected groups.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor marine police foils attempt to smuggle nearly 700,000 lobster fry to Singapore
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The Johor Region Two Marine Police Force (PPM) foiled an attempt to smuggle nearly 700,000 lobster fry worth about RM13.4 million to Singapore via sea at Tanjung Belungkor yesterday afternoon.
Region Two marine police commander assistant commissioner Noor Azman Jamal said the seizure was carried out by a team from the Johor Marine Intelligence Unit in collaboration with the state Fisheries Department after receiving information on the smuggling activity at about 6.30pm.
He said an inspection on an Isuzu lorry at an illegal jetty led to the discovery of the lobster fry, which were stored in white polystyrene boxes at the rear of the vehicle.
"Inspection found 124 white polystyrene boxes containing transparent plastic bags filled with an estimated 671,600 lobster fry.
"Three local male workers aged between 31 and 57 were also detained for failing to produce valid export documents," he said in a statement today.
He said the suspects and seized items, including the lorry, were taken to the state Fisheries Department office for documentation and further action, and the case is being investigated under Section 40 of the Fisheries Act 1985.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rally crowd halts near Jalan TAR–Jalan Tun Perak junction as police form cordon
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The crowd participating in the anti-Azam Baki rally has halted just before the Jalan Tuanku Abdul Rahman–Jalan Tun Perak junction, a short distance from Dataran Merdeka.
Participants, who had earlier marched from the Sogo shopping complex, were seen gathering at the junction as police maintained a presence to monitor the situation.
Among those spotted at the rally were former Economy Minister Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli; activist Albert Tei; Teoh Lee Lan, sister of the late Teoh Beng Hock; and Parti Sosialis Malaysia (PSM) deputy chairperson S. Arutchelvan.
Police also formed a cordon along Jalan Raja, just before Dataran Merdeka, effectively blocking access to the square as the crowd remained stationed nearby.
The situation remained under control, with officers standing guard while chants and speeches continued intermittently at the junction.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar: Azam Baki performed duties well, now time for new MACC leadership
Article:
BUKIT MERTAJAM: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim extended his appreciation for outgoing Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Chief Commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki, while voicing confidence in his successor to steer the agency forward.
Anwar said Azam had carried out his duties to the best of his ability throughout his tenure helming the anti-graft body.
"First, we record our appreciation to Tan Sri Azam Baki, who performed his responsibilities as well as possible. Now, it is the turn of the new chief commissioner," he told reporters after launching the NADI Kubang Semang at Yayasan Aman here this afternoon.
Former High Court Judge Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman has been appointed as the new MACC Chief Commissioner, effective May 13.
Anwar noted that while it was too early to fully assess the new appointee, several key factors supported the decision.
"He comes from the judiciary and is not involved in any major business interests or political parties. I believe he is suitable to lead the MACC and continue strengthening its capabilities," he said.
Anwar added that the appointment was discussed with His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, with a two-year term agreed upon.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 41 heat-related cases recorded so far - Dzulkefly
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A total of 41 heat-related cases have been recorded so far, with the majority involving heat exhaustion, said Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad.
He said that of the total, 35 cases involved heat exhaustion, followed by three cases of heatstroke due to physical activity, two cases of heatstroke indoors, and one case of heat cramps.
"However, as of yesterday (April 24), no new heat-related cases have been reported," he told reporters after officiating the launch of the 'Mai Kurang' Recipe Book and the 'Rewang Bersama Komuniti' programme at the Tun Abdul Razak Memorial here today.
As of 6am today, 13 districts in five states recorded Level 1 hot weather, or the alert category, involving Perlis, Kedah (Kubang Pasu, Pokok Sena, Pendang, Padang Terap and Sik), Kelantan (Jeli, Tanah Merah, Pasir Mas and Kuala Krai), Perak (Kuala Kangsar and Hulu Perak), as well as Jerantut in Pahang.
In addition, he said that Machang district in Kelantan recorded Level 2 hot weather, or a heatwave.
Meanwhile, in another development, Dzulkefly expressed support for Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's proposal to limit the movement of Cabinet members and ministries in response to the global supply crisis.
He said the measure is in line with the Madani government's approach, which emphasises prudent financial management, particularly in addressing current challenges.
"Immediate measures need to be considered to ensure Malaysia does not face energy rationing as seen in several other countries," he said.
Yesterday, Anwar said the government is refining more drastic measures to reduce energy consumption following the
global supply crisis
, including considering limiting the movement of Cabinet members and ministries.
The prime minister said such immediate action is critical to ensure Malaysia does not fall into an energy rationing situation as experienced by several other countries, adding that a final decision will be made at the National Economic Action Council (MTEN) meeting.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Iran defence ministry says US seeks 'face-saving' way out of war
Article:
TEHERAN: Iran's defence ministry on Saturday said the US was seeking a "face-saving" way to exit the war, as US envoys travelled to Pakistan for peace negotiations.
"Our military power today is a dominant force, and the enemy is looking for a face-saving way to escape the war quagmire it has become trapped in," media outlet ISNA quoted a ministry spokesperson as saying.
It comes as emissaries Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner make their way to Islamabad, though Iranian state media has said direct negotiations are not on the cards.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Gobind, Wee call on new MACC chief to present reforms, restore public trust
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: DAP national chairman Gobind Singh Deo has called on the newly-appointed Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief to outline his vision for the commission.
He said Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman must present a clear roadmap of the specific reforms he intends to implement to safeguard the commission's independence and prevent future abuses of power.
"His immediate priority must be to restore public confidence in the MACC.
"This requires addressing allegations of 'corporate mafia' activities surrounding the agency and resolving the shareholding controversy involving his predecessor, Tan Sri Azam Baki.
"Regarding the corporate mafia allegations, questions have been raised about the suspected involvement of certain MACC officials in corporate manoeuvres during investigations," he said in a statement.
Azam has faced numerous calls to step down over share ownership and allegations of links to a purported "corporate mafia". He has denied any wrongdoing.
Gobind said the claims must be addressed without fear, favour or delay, with clear timelines set for completion.
"Concerning the shareholding issue, matters remain pending despite the Cabinet directing action over a month ago. While Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar said that a formal process must be followed, this cannot continue indefinitely."
He said Halim should begin his tenure without such lingering allegations.
"As a special committee led by the Attorney-General has already presented its findings to the cabinet, this matter must be finalised quickly.
"Ultimately, accountability and transparency are paramount. The initial steps taken by the new MACC chief will define the trajectory of its leadership. To restore public trust, these pressing issues must be addressed effectively to signal that genuine reforms are under way."
Meanwhile, MCA president Datuk Seri Dr Wee Ka Siong said Halim's distinguished judicial background brings a strong foundation in evidence, due process and procedural fairness, qualities essential in strengthening public confidence in the commission's work.
"At this important juncture, the rakyat look to the MACC not only for enforcement, but for leadership in upholding the highest standards of integrity, independence and accountability.
"This appointment presents an opportunity to reinforce those values and to further elevate the institution."
He said members of the public, including MCA Youth, have taken to the streets to make their voices heard.
"This demonstrates the depth of public concern over the integrity and direction of the MACC. We call on the incoming chief to take these concerns seriously and to engage openly with the rakyat as a priority upon assuming office."
Wee added that continued efforts towards institutional reforms are key, with a clear expectation that the MACC remains meaningfully answerable to Parliament, supported by robust oversight mechanisms to ensure transparency, accountability and public trust.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Govt aims to cut NRW to 28pct under National Water Policy
Article:
ALOR GAJAH: The implementation of the National Water Policy will focus on key challenges in the water sector, including reducing the high level of non-revenue water (NRW) at the national level.
Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof said the current average NRW rate stands at about 33.4 per cent, and the government aims to reduce it to 28 per cent through a more comprehensive and integrated approach to ensure the country does not face water scarcity issues as experienced by some countries.
He said the policy encompasses planning at both federal and state levels, while emphasising the overall management of water resources, including distribution and supply system efficiency.
"The national water policy involves planning at both the federal and state levels. We are looking at the overall water ecosystem and how to manage this resource more efficiently, including addressing the challenges of NRW.
"We want to ensure that water is safe, clean and, most importantly, affordable. That is why we must also protect our water resources, as we do not want a situation where the country has water but requires extensive processing, making it expensive," he said at a press conference here today.
Earlier, Fadillah, who is also energy transition and water transformation minister, launched the national-level World Water Day 2026 celebration at Tanjung Bidara, Masjid Tanah, which was also attended by Melaka Chief Minister Datuk Seri Ab Rauf Yusoh.
Fadillah said among the measures taken is the provision of matching grants to states that successfully reduce NRW rate below the set threshold, based on their level of achievement.
"This means states must first implement the measures, and if they succeed in reducing NRW levels, the government will reimburse the allocation based on their percentage of achievement. If they achieve 75 per cent, we will return 75 per cent; if 50 per cent, then 50 per cent," he said.
He said states recording high NRW levels include Kelantan, Kedah, Sabah and Sarawak.
Meanwhile, he said the government will establish a special task force to monitor the implementation of all water-related projects to ensure they are carried out more effectively.
"We are aware there are challenges in project implementation, so this task force will ensure close monitoring and that any issues can be resolved promptly," he said.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hybrid work shift boosts demand for co-working spaces
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Co-working operators are preparing to meet Malaysia's shift towards hybrid work arrangements, as demand grows for flexible workspace solutions.
Flexible workspace provider WORQ said the trend towards hybrid and flexible work models has moved from a temporary response to a long-term structural shift, particularly in the Klang Valley.
Its management said companies are increasingly adopting hybrid arrangements to balance productivity, cost efficiency, employee retention and well-being.
"Over the past few years, flexible work has transitioned into a long-term operating model, with more organisations formalising hybrid structures as part of their business strategy," it told the New Straits Times.
The company said co-working operators are supporting businesses, particularly small and medium enterprises (SMEs), by providing ready-to-use workspaces without long-term leases or heavy upfront costs.
"For SMEs, this offers immediate access to professional environments, while larger corporations can use co-working spaces to scale operations or support distributed teams."
WORQ, which operates 13 locations across the Klang Valley, said its workspaces are aligned with transit-oriented development (TOD) networks, allowing professionals to work closer to home while remaining connected to key commercial hubs.
It added that the government's push for work-from-home (WFH) arrangements is expected to reinforce the shift and give businesses greater confidence to adopt hybrid work on a permanent basis.
To support this shift, WORQ has launched its "Work Closer, Work Better" campaign, offering trial access and discounted memberships to encourage professionals to use flexible workspaces nearer to where they live.
The company said the move towards hybrid work is likely to increase demand for co-working spaces rather than reduce overall office usage, as companies rethink traditional workplace structures.
"While some firms may scale down centralised office space, demand is shifting towards decentralised, flexible workspaces that are easily accessible," it said.
It noted that new locations within its network have seen strong pre-commitments, particularly in well-connected urban hubs, highlighting the importance of accessibility in a hybrid work environment.
WORQ said many organisations are adopting a hub-and-spoke model, combining a central headquarters with smaller satellite offices or flexible workspaces across multiple locations.
Under this model, co-working spaces play a role in reducing commuting time while maintaining productivity and connectivity.
Beyond physical space, WORQ said operators are increasingly focusing on employee experience, including integrating wellness and lifestyle elements into the workplace.
It cited its "WORQ Well" concept, which includes facilities such as fitness amenities and wellness programmes to promote a more balanced and sustainable work environment.
"As hybrid work becomes the norm, co-working spaces are evolving into a 'third space' between home and headquarters, supporting flexibility while fostering collaboration and community."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Over half a million adults suffer from four NCDs
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is facing a worrying public health crisis as non-communicable diseases (NCDs) such as diabetes, hypertension, obesity and heart disease continue to pose a major threat to public health.
Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said that based on the National Health and Morbidity Survey (NHMS), more than half a million adults in the country suffer from at least four major NCDs simultaneously.
He said statistics also show that one in six adults has diabetes, while one in three suffers from high blood pressure.
"This situation is not merely a personal health issue, but also has a significant impact on the national economy, which is estimated to incur losses of about RM64.2 billion annually due to NCDs," he said when speaking at the launch of the 'Mai Kurang' Recipe Book and the 'Rewang Bersama Komuniti' programme at the Tun Abdul Razak Memorial today.
He said among the main causes of the rise in NCDs are unhealthy dietary habits among Malaysians, including excessive intake of sugar and salt.
"NHMS 2024 data show that 47 per cent of adults consume sugar beyond the recommended level, while three out of four adults consume excessive salt," he said.
He said the situation requires a more comprehensive approach to change public health behaviour, not merely awareness campaigns.
In this regard, he said the  Health Ministry (MOH) is now focusing on a choice architecture approach to encourage healthier food choices among the public.
Meanwhile, he said the launch of the 'Mai Kurang' Recipe Book is one of the key interventions to support the effort.
"This book serves as an intervention tool that makes it easier for the public to prepare healthy meals that are lower in sugar, salt and calories in a simple way while meeting local tastes," he said.
Dzulkefly said efforts to address NCDs require a shift from a treatment-based approach to one focused on prevention and wellness, which requires the cooperation of all parties, including the private sector, communities and individuals.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Alleged NGO fund misuse highlights need for stronger regulation
Article:
LETTERS:
We view with grave concern the investigation conducted by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) into the alleged misappropriation of approximately RM230 million involving a non-governmental organisation (NGO). This matter is deeply troubling as it involves public funds entrusted in good faith to support charitable causes and assist those in need.
Incidents of this nature not only tarnish the image of NGOs as a whole but also significantly undermine public confidence in charitable giving. If such issues are not addressed decisively and transparently, it may discourage the public from contributing to genuine humanitarian efforts, ultimately affecting the most vulnerable groups in society.
In light of this, we urge the relevant authorities to consider the following measures:
1. Strengthening regulations and oversight
The government should introduce stricter guidelines governing the collection, management, and utilisation of public donations by NGOs, supported by robust and continuous monitoring mechanisms.
2. Enhancing transparency and accountability
All NGOs that raise public funds must be required to produce regularly audited financial statements and make them accessible to the public to ensure full transparency.
3. Establishing a dedicated regulatory framework
Consideration should be given to establishing a dedicated regulatory body, or strengthening existing institutions, to oversee NGO activities, particularly in relation to public fundraising.
4. Introducing specific legislation
It may be timely to review and enact specific laws to regulate NGO operations, especially concerning the collection and use of public funds, to safeguard the interests of donors and beneficiaries alike.
5. Firm action against offenders
Any individual or organisation found guilty of misappropriating public funds must face firm legal action as a deterrent and to uphold justice.
This issue is fundamentally about trust. Donations made by the public are a reflection of their confidence in the integrity and mission of NGOs. Any breach of this trust is not only a betrayal of donors but also a grave injustice to the intended beneficiaries.
At the same time, we must not allow the misconduct of a few "bad apples" to undermine the credibility of the many NGOs that operate with integrity and genuine commitment. Meaningful reforms are therefore essential to ensure that the NGO sector remains trusted, respected, and effective in serving society.
TAN SRI LEE LAM THYE
MEMBER OF INTEGRITY
INSTITUTE
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Azam Baki rally continues after brief fireworks disruption
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Protesters at a rally against Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki resumed a procession towards Dataran Merdeka along Jalan Tuanku Abdul Rahman after a brief disruption.
Participants were seen regrouping after the fireworks incident and continuing their march, with some chanting slogans directed at Azam as they moved forward.
Police personnel escorted the procession and monitored the situation closely to maintain public order as the crowd moved towards the city centre.
The atmosphere, while more controlled, remained tense as the rally continued along the main thoroughfare.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MRSM bullies identified, says Asyraf Wajdi
Article:
JASIN: Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) has identified the perpetrators involved in the bullying case at Sultan Azlan Shah Maktab Rendah Sains Mara (MRSM) in Perak, which went viral on social media.
Its chairman Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said investigations into the case are in the final stages before stern disciplinary action is taken against those responsible.
Asyraf said the victim's family has provided information, and the MRSM management has submitted a formal report on the incident.
"We will not compromise on any form of bullying.
"Mara's policy is clear: 'You touch, you go.' Even if there are appeals, I will not entertain them," he said to reporters here today.
However, Asyraf declined to reveal the number of students involved, saying that details would be released once the full investigation is completed.
The case came to light following a complaint by a Facebook user, Khairul Nizam, regarding the issue of bullying at the school.
Khairul Nizam claimed that the bullies cut the victim's uniform and sent anonymous letters and threats, in addition to vandalism and mental abuse.
He also claimed that at the time he wrote the post, no firm action had been taken against the perpetrators although a report had been lodged with the MRSM management.
Asyraf, who saw Khairul Nizam's social media post, immediately instructed
Mara to look into the case and take action
.
In a bid to curb both physical and mental bullying, Asyraf announced that Mara is developing a specialised training module in collaboration with Universiti Putra Malaysia (UPM) for its new intake of ex-military wardens.
He said the move is necessary as bullying trends have evolved to include psychological elements, which are often harder to detect than physical ones.
"We hope this step will reduce bullying cases across the board, whether physical or mental," he said.
On a separate matter, Asyraf announced an allocation of RM650,000 to upgrade the infrastructure at Tun Ghafar Baba MRSM.
The allocation comes after the institution made history as the top-performing school in the 2025 Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) examinations.
He said the funds are aimed at improving basic facilities at the campus, which is one of the oldest in the country, having been established over five decades ago.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police smash subsidised diesel smuggling syndicate, arrest eight
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police have smashed a subsidised diesel smuggling syndicate with the arrest of eight individuals in Op Taring Bravo 1 at three factories in Shah Alam and Sungai Buloh yesterday.
Wildlife Crime Bureau/Intelligence and Special Investigation (WCB/PSK) commander Datuk Mohd Zaki Ashar said 124,950 litres of subsidised diesel worth RM645,632 were seized during the 3.50pm raid.
He said the eight individuals arrested comprised six locals and two foreigners, aged between 27 and 56, who played various roles, including security guard, factory supervisor, manager, storekeeper, workers, as well as tanker lorry attendant and driver.
"The syndicate carried out subsidised diesel diversion activities in a sophisticated and organised manner by manipulating the supply chain to reap substantial profits.
"More concerning was that the syndicate also operated aggressively by reselling the diesel into the industrial market at significantly higher prices," he said in a statement today.
Mohd Zaki said the syndicate, active since early this year, collected subsidised diesel in stages around Selangor before smuggling it out in bulk for sale at RM4 per litre in 5,000-litre quantities to third parties acting as collection and storage centres.
He added that the diesel was then resold and distributed daily in volumes of 5,000 to 6,000 litres at between RM4.80 and RM5.00 per litre to several industrial and construction companies in the Klang Valley.
"The operation, which also involved Shah Alam City Council (MBSA) and Petaling Jaya City Council (MBPJ), led to the seizure of two tanker lorries, 10 intermediate bulk containers (IBCs), six pump machines with hoses and 12 skid tanks, bringing the total value of seizures to RM1,827,712," he said.
Mohd Zaki said all seized items, suspects and confiscated subsidised diesel had been handed over to the Selangor Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry (KPDN) for further investigation under Section 21 of the Control of Supplies Act 1961.
He said the two foreign nationals arrested had been handed over to the Sungai Buloh police headquarters for further investigation under the Immigration Act 1959/63 and Regulation 39(b) of the Immigration Regulations 1963.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Loke: Mechanism for diesel road tax reduction proposed
Article:
BATU KAWAN: The Transport Ministry has proposed a mechanism to the National Economic Action Council (NEAC) secretariat to reduce road tax rates for diesel vehicles to help ease the public burden following rising fuel costs.
Its Minister Anthony Loke said the proposal is in line with government efforts to adopt a more comprehensive approach to assist diesel vehicle owners affected by changes in fuel prices.
He said diesel prices are currently set on a floating basis to ensure market transparency and curb leakages and smuggling activities.
"Our policy is to float diesel prices, except for groups receiving subsidies, as setting prices below market levels would lead to more smuggling.
"To help diesel vehicle owners, we currently provide the Budi Madani individual subsidy of RM200 a month, while bus and lorry operators receive subsidies through the fleet card system.
"For those who do not receive such assistance, we are looking at ways to help, including reducing road tax," he said at the 80th anniversary celebration of the Road Transport Department (RTD) and the RTD Madani Mega Carnival at the State Stadium grounds here today.
The event was launched by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and was also attended by Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow, Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar and RTD director-general Datuk Aedy Fadly Ramli.
Also launched at the event was the new version of the MyJPJ mobile application, an integrated platform offering various RTD services in a more modern, efficient and user-friendly format.
Singer Datuk Seri Siti Nurhaliza Tarudin was also named 'Road Safety Icon'.
Loke said further studies are being carried out to assess the implications of the proposal, including the appropriate reduction rate and its impact on government revenue, with an announcement expected soon.
Earlier, Anwar said the government is considering reducing road tax for diesel vehicles in the near future to ease the burden on the public following the increase in fuel prices.
He said the Transport Ministry has been tasked with identifying a suitable mechanism, taking into account the impact of higher diesel prices on affected users, particularly sectors that still rely on diesel vehicles.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RM230mil NGO fund probe: Govt urged to strengthen regulation
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government is urged to take swift measures to strengthen regulation and prevent misconduct among non-governmental organisations (NGOs) following the investigation into the alleged misappropriation of approximately RM230 million involving an NGO, said social activist Tan Sri Lee Lam Thye.
The Alliance for a Safe Community chairman said meaningful reforms are essential to ensure that the NGO sector remains trusted, respected and effective in serving society.
He suggests that the government introduce stricter guidelines governing the collection, management and utilisation of public donations by NGOs, supported by robust and continuous monitoring mechanisms.
"All NGOs that raise public funds must be required to produce regularly audited financial statements and make them accessible to the public to ensure full transparency.
"Consideration should be given to establishing a dedicated regulatory body, or strengthening existing institutions, to oversee NGO activities, particularly in relation to public fundraising," he said in a statement today.
He suggested to review and enact specific laws to regulate NGO operations, especially concerning the collection and use of public funds, to safeguard the interests of donors and beneficiaries alike.
Any individual or organisation found guilty of misappropriating public funds must face firm legal action as a deterrent and to uphold justice, Lee said.
Reflecting on the recent case, he said the incidents not only tarnish the image of NGOs as a whole but also significantly undermine public confidence in charitable giving.
"Any breach of this trust is not only a betrayal of donors but also a grave injustice to the intended beneficiaries,
"If such issues are not addressed decisively and transparently, it may discourage the public from contributing to genuine humanitarian efforts, ultimately affecting the most vulnerable groups in society," Lee added.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Chaos at anti-Azam Baki rally as fireworks go off [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A brief moment of chaos broke out at a protest rally against Tan Sri Azam Baki today after fireworks were set off near the Sogo shopping complex.
The incident, which occurred during a series of speeches, caused the crowd to scatter approximately 50m away from the initial gathering point.
One person is believed to have been injured in the commotion and was seen covering her eyes after the fireworks went off.
Police then moved in to cordon off the area.
Despite the disruption, the situation was quickly brought under control and the rally resumed.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Azam Baki shareholding probe completed, decision with Chief Secretary
Article:
BUKIT MERTAJAM: Investigations into allegations involving share ownership linked to Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki have been completed and are awaiting a final decision at the highest level of government.
Unity Government spokesman Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the matter was no longer in the investigative stage, with the next step now being handled administratively by the Chief Secretary to the Government, Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar.
"To my understanding, if what is being referred to is the investigation into the allegations on shareholding, that has been completed," he told newsmen at Nadi Kubang Semang here today.
He said the findings from the special investigation committee had been presented to the Cabinet, and the next course of action would be determined by the Chief Secretary.
"The subsequent process is now being managed by the Chief Secretary to the Government.
"The final decision will be based on what has been presented to the Cabinet," he added.
Prior to this, the government had established a special committee to examine the allegations related to
Azam's share ownership
, which were first reported following claims by international media outlet Bloomberg.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Roadblocks set up near LTSIP ahead of motorcycle convoy
Article:
KOTA BARU: Roadblocks will be carried out this afternoon near Sultan Ismail Petra Airport (LTSIP) in Kota Baru, Kelantan, with the Road Transport Department (RTD) working together with traffic police to inspect all motorcycles entering the airport area.
Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the enforcement action follows a planned motorcycle convoy involving several assemblymen to the airport, which he described as irresponsible due to potential disruption to passenger movement and airport operations.
"The airport area is a strategic zone, and security must be maintained at all times to ensure smooth and uninterrupted passenger travel.
"If any congestion causes passengers to miss their flights, will the organisers take responsibility for compensating their flight tickets?
"The actions of several state assemblymen in organising a Mat Motor convoy to LTSIP this evening are highly irresponsible," he said in a Facebook post.
He said that, to ensure the safety and smooth operation of the airport, he had instructed the RTD to work with traffic police to carry out roadblocks and inspect all motorcycles entering the airport area without exception, including those ridden by assemblymen.
He said any motorcycle convoy should obtain approval from the relevant authorities.
"Have the organisers obtained approval?" he questioned.
According to the poster, the convoy, 'Konvoi & Lepak Mat Motor Bersama YB YB', is organised by the Backbenchers Club (BBC) and Kelantan Pas Youth.
The convoy will begin with Asar prayers at Masjid Muhammadi, followed by a speech by the BBC president.
It will then proceed from Kota Lama towards LTSIP, pass through Kota Bharu town and end behind Aeon Mall.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Community-run centres bridge education gap in Sabah
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: In today's modern world, there are often doubts about the quality of education provided by unofficial learning institutions.
However, Pacos Trust executive director Anne Lasimbang said the organisation's initiative to establish Community Learning Centres (CLCs) had brought significant changes to children's lives.
Before the community-based organisation started the CLCs in 1993, Anne found that many pupils in rural areas were falling behind.
"When they entered primary school, they were often still unable to read and write. Because of this, they lost interest in learning.
"For them, going to school was very difficult. Their homes were far from school, and when they were in class, they struggled to understand what teachers were saying.
"One of the ways to address this was through early childhood education," she said in a 30-minute documentary titled "Mongingia", produced by local production company Third Rice Culture.
"Mongingia", which means "teacher" in the Kadazan-Dusun language, traces three decades of community-based early education in rural Sabah.
To date, 25 CLCs have been established across Sabah.
Apart from the basic formal education syllabus, the centres also incorporate additional learning such as native languages, local food, traditions, and community skills, including weaving and jewellery-making.
Gloriana Jicob is among the success stories, having become a teacher at Sekolah Kebangsaan Kuamut in Kinabatangan.
"Without the CLC intervention, I don't think I would have become a teacher. I was inspired by my CLC teacher," she said in the documentary.
Although the CLCs were initiated by Pacos Trust, they are largely run by the communities themselves, particularly women with basic education, often operating from makeshift buildings.
One such educator is NorIizah Stephen, 41, who has served at CLC Saguon in Tongod since 2012.
"I never thought I would last this long, especially as I faced a lot of criticism throughout the journey, particularly about my educational background.
"But what kept me going is turning those negative words into motivation to prove that we can do it," she said during a sharing session after Yayasan DayaDiri (YDD) hosted the screening of "Mongingia" at Universiti Malaysia Sabah here yesterday.
YDD partnered with Pacos Trust from 2010 to 2019, expanding CLCs from six to 25 centres across Sabah and Sarawak, benefiting thousands through training, leadership development and community ownership.
To date, CLCs have yet to receive recognition from the government.
Also present at the screening was YDD chairman Shahrin Merican.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: No compromise: Asyraf Wajdi calls for action over bullying at MRSM
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) management has been instructed to take firm action over a bullying complaint involving Maktab Rendah Sains Mara (MRSM) Sultan Azlan Shah in Kuala Kangar.
Mara chairman Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said there would be no compromise on bullying at MRSM.
"I have instructed the Mara management to take firm action. No compromise. There is no place for bullies in MRSM," he said in the comments section of a post on his Facebook page today.
He was responding to a complaint by a Facebook user named Khairul Nizam on the issue of bullying at the school.
Khairul Nizam claimed that although a report had been lodged with the MRSM management, no firm action had been taken against the perpetrators, resulting in the victim continuing to face bullying and threats.
He also claimed that nine students had been identified as suspects, while the victim is a Form One student.
He claimed that the bullies had cut the victim and sent anonymous letters and threats, in addition to vandalism and mental abuse.
"The bullying began in early 2026. About two months ago, a report was made to the MRSM management, but no concrete action was taken.
"The counsellor has called in the victim and the nine students suspected of bullying. However, the victim continues to be bullied and lives in fear.
"Most recently, the victim received threats," the comment read.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: No permit issued for motorcycle convoy, say Kelantan cops
Article:
KOTA BARU: Kelantan police have not issued a permit to the organiser of a planned motorcycle convoy scheduled to be held today at several locations, including Sultan Ismail Petra Airport.
State police chief Mohd Yusoff Mamat warned that action would be taken against those who proceed with the convoy.
Yusoff said he had contacted the organiser last night to explain the regulations governing such events.
"As of 9.30pm, the organiser gave his assurance that the convoy will not proceed to the airport in Pengkalan Chepa.
"Instead, they will ride at several other locations," he said when contacted.
However, Yusoff stressed that police would not tolerate any parties causing
disruption, particularly along routes leading to the airport.
"This is especially critical as many haj pilgrims are travelling to the airport. Any traffic congestion could affect their movement," he said.
Despite the assurance, he said police have been instructed to set up roadblocks this evening to monitor the situation and ensure compliance.
He added that police would also cooperate with the state Road Transport Department (RTD) to monitor and enforce regulations throughout the operation.
"I think the organiser is trying to create a problem or provoke the authorities over this issue," he said.
It has been widely circulated on social media since yesterday that a motorcycle convoy involving several state assemblymen would be held this evening, with plans to head towards the airport.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Palestinians vote in first elections since Gaza war
Article:
RAMALLAH: Palestinians in the West Bank and a central area of Gaza began voting Saturday in municipal elections in the first vote since the Gaza war, marked by a narrow political field and widespread disillusionment.
Nearly 1.5 million people are registered to vote in the Israeli-occupied West Bank, as well as 70,000 people in Gaza's Deir el-Balah area, according to the Ramallah-based Central Elections Commission.
Polling stations opened at 7am (0400 GMT).
AFP footage from Al-Bireh in the West Bank and Deir el-Balah in Gaza showed election officials in polling stations as Palestinians came to cast ballots.
Most electoral lists are aligned with president Mahmud Abbas's secular-nationalist Fatah party or feature candidates running as independents.
There are no lists affiliated with Fatah's archrival Hamas, which controls nearly half of the Gaza Strip.
In most cities, Fatah-backed tickets will run against independent lists headed by candidates from factions such as the Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine (Marxist-Leninist).
"We must see change every four years through elections... We can't change the situation but we hope to replace people... people who might be better and help develop the community," said Khalid Eid, 55, after he voted in Al-Bireh.
Municipal councils are responsible for basic services such as water, sanitation and local infrastructure and do not enact legislation.
The Palestinian Authority faces widespread criticism over corruption, stagnation and declining legitimacy.
Western and regional donors have increasingly tied financial and diplomatic support to visible reforms, particularly at the local governance level, as national elections remain frozen.
With no presidential or legislative elections held since 2006, municipal councils have become one of the few functioning democratic institutions under PA administration.
UN coordinator Ramiz Alakbarov commended the election commission for organising a "credible process."
"Saturday's elections represent an important opportunity for Palestinians to exercise their democratic rights during an exceptionally challenging period," Alakbarov said in a statement ahead of the polls.
[[nid:1425784]]
Mahmud Bader, a businessman from the northern West Bank city of Tulkarem, where two adjacent refugee camps have been under Israeli military control for over a year, said he would vote despite having little hope for meaningful change.
"Whether candidates are independent or partisan, it has no effect and will have no effect or benefit for the city," he told AFP on Friday.
"The (Israeli) occupation is the one that rules Tulkarem. It would only be an image shown to the international media – as if we have elections, a state or independence."
Polling stations in the West Bank will close at 7 pm, while polls in Deir el-Balah will close at 5 pm to facilitate counting in daylight due to the lack of electricity in the war-devastated strip, the elections commission told AFP.
Two years of war that started in October 2023 have left swathes of Gaza destroyed and more than 72,000 people dead, according to the territory's health ministry, whose figures are considered reliable by the UN.
Public infrastructure, sanitation services and the health sector are struggling to function.
'STRONG DETERMINATION'
Gaza, which has been under Hamas control since 2007, is holding its first vote since legislative elections of 2006 that the Islamist movement won.
The Palestinian Authority is holding elections only in Deir el-Balah "as an experiment (to test its own) success or failure, since there are no post-war opinion polls", Jamal al-Fadi, a political scientist at Cairo's Al-Azhar University, told AFP.
Deir el-Balah was chosen as it was one of the only places in Gaza where "the population has remained largely in place and not been displaced" by more than two years of war between Hamas and Israel, Fadi said.
The election commission has recruited polling staff from civil society organisations and hired "a private security company to secure polling centres" for the Gaza vote, its spokesman Fareed Taamallah told AFP.
Mohammed al-Hasayna, 24, said after voting in Deir el-Balah that although the elections were largely symbolic, they served as a sign of people's "will to live."
"We are an educated people with strong determination, and we deserve to have our own state," he told AFP.
"We want the world to help us overcome the catastrophe of war. Enough wars – it is time to work towards rebuilding Gaza."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Woman suffers miscarriage, broken arm after alleged assault by husband
Article:
SUNGAI PETANI: A 39-year-old woman suffered a miscarriage after she was allegedly beaten by her husband at their home in Ambangan Heights here on Thursday.
The victim, who was 12 weeks pregnant, is being treated at a hospital for multiple injuries, including a broken arm and suspected fractured ribs.
The victim's brother said the couple married in Thailand last December but had yet to register the union in Malaysia. He claimed his sister, a civil servant, had been frequently assaulted throughout the marriage.
In the 1pm incident, a dispute reportedly escalated into an assault where the suspect allegedly used clothes hangers, a garden hose, a curtain rod, and a broomstick to beat the victim.
The man also allegedly punched her in the stomach and beat her until the broomstick broke.
"Her left hand and fingers were broken, and her face was badly bruised," the brother said when contacted.
He added that the suspect had threatened the family, including the victim's two children from a previous marriage. The man also allegedly fled with the victim's jewellery and cash amounting to nearly RM50,000.
"My sister is still traumatised. Tomorrow is her birthday, but instead of celebrating, she is in the hospital in fear," he said.
The victim's brother and mother have lodged a report at the Kuala Muda police headquarters.
Kuala Muda police chief Assistant Commissioner Hanyan Ramlan confirmed receiving the report.
He said the case is being investigated under Section 323 of the Penal Code for voluntarily causing hurt and Section 18 of the Domestic Violence Act.
The offence is punishable by a 1-year jail term or RM2,000 fine, or both.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sogo area calm ahead of planned Azam Baki rally
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police personnel were seen leaving the Dang Wangi district police headquarters and heading towards the Sogo shopping complex area today, where demonstrators were expected to gather for the Himpunan Tangkap Azam Baki.
A check around Jalan Tuanku Abdul Rahman near Sogo, however, found the area remained calm and unusually quiet despite earlier expectations of a crowd forming.
As of 1.30pm, there were no signs of individuals or groups assembling, nor anyone carrying placards in the vicinity.
Members of the public present were mainly shoppers and workers going about their daily routines, with business activity continuing as usual without disruption.
Several police personnel were stationed around the area as part of monitoring and control measures to maintain public order and prevent any untoward incidents.
Although no movement linked to the rally was detected at the location, authorities appeared to remain on standby for any developments.
At Dataran Merdeka, several police vehicles were also deployed as a precautionary measure to maintain security.
The Tangkap Azam Baki secretariat had earlier announced plans to hold the gathering today at Dataran Merdeka, calling for a transparent investigation into allegations involving Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Chief Commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki, including issues related to share ownership and claims of a so-called "corporate mafia" within enforcement agencies.
Separately, Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar announced that High Court judge Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman has been appointed as the new MACC chief commissioner, effective May 13, with the consent of the Yang di-Pertuan Agong.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MetMalaysia issues thunderstorm warning across several states
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning, with heavy rain and strong winds expected across six states and a federal territory until 4pm today.
Areas expected to be affected include Selangor (Kuala Selangor, Klang, Gombak, Petaling and Hulu Langat) and Kuala Lumpur.
Other areas include Pahang (Maran, Bera, Pekan and Rompin), Negri Sembilan (Jempol and Tampin), Melaka (Jasin) and Johor (Tangkak, Segamat, Muar, Batu Pahat, Kluang, Pontian and Kulai).
In Sabah, the warning covers Tawau.
According to the department, the warning is issued when there are signs of thunderstorm activity with rainfall exceeding 20 millimetres per hour, either occurring or expected to persist for more than an hour.
MetMalaysia said the short-term warning is valid for a period not exceeding six hours from the time it is issued.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Govt to update zakat guidelines to strengthen governance
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government will update the guidelines on the zakat system to further strengthen governance and address emerging concerns, said Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Religious Affairs) Dr Zulkifli Hasan.
He said while existing frameworks and references were already in place, there was room to enhance them to ensure the zakat system remained robust, transparent and responsive.
"Everything is already guided by established references, but we are looking at ways to strengthen what we have.
"This is part of a continuous process to improve and reinforce our institutions over time," he told reporters after officiating the Barakah Pit Stop programme at the Federal Territories Mosque.
Zulkifli said the government remained confident in the country's zakat institutions, as Malaysia's system had received international recognition and multiple awards.
He said zakat management in the country had also been commended by the Auditor-General, with distributions carried out in an orderly manner and in accordance with proper governance.
"From the aspect of distribution, it is implemented with discipline and good governance.
"There is also transparency, where the public can verify how contributions are managed," he said.
On Tuesday, three people, including the deputy chairman of an non-governmental organisation (NGO), were detained by the MACC on suspicion of misappropriating approximately RM230 million.
The next day, the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) clarified that the amount does not involve funds from zakat institutions.
However, the misappropriated funds included collections that were meant to be channelled to a state zakat body.
Addressing concerns regarding online zakat payments and the presence of third-party agents, he said the matter was handled according to established procedures and professional standards.
"Any appointment or mechanism involved follows proper governance and professionalism.
"These are not claims made by us alone, but are supported by audits and established systems," he added.
The agents, or 'amil', are individuals or groups officially appointed by authorities to assist in zakat collections and distributions.
The public can verify the agents through state zakat institutions' official website, where a list of appointed names is provided.
He also told the public to continue placing trust in official zakat institutions, emphasising their role as a benchmark not only domestically but internationally.
"We remain fully confident in our zakat institutions, and we hope Malaysians will continue to support and trust them as we strive to do our best," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: First female Archbishop of Canterbury to meet Pope Leo
Article:
VATICAN CITY: New Archbishop of Canterbury Sarah Mullally, the first woman to lead the world's Anglican Christians, heads to Rome and the Vatican this weekend for an audience with Pope Leo XIV.
Mullally will meet the head of the world's 1.4 billion Catholics during a four-day visit starting Saturday, her first abroad since being enthroned last month as the Church of England's top cleric.
The audience comes 60 years after a historic meeting in 1966 between then archbishop Michael Ramsey and pope Paul VI, the first at that level since the Church of England was created in the 16th century, when king Henry VIII broke with Rome.
Relations have been steadily improving since although in 2016 their successors noted "new disagreements", particularly on the ordination of the women – making Mullally's visit a significant moment.
The 63-year-old former nurse, who is married with two children, is the first woman to lead the mother church of the world's 85-million strong Anglican community.
The first female Anglican bishops were appointed in the United States in 1989, and they have been allowed in the Church of England since 2014, although the issue remains divisive.
By contrast the Catholic Church has repeatedly rejected the idea of female priests, while male priests also cannot marry – with the exception of married Anglican priests who want to convert.
Campaigners for women's rights, who had hoped for progress under former Pope Francis before his death last year, welcomed the archbishop's visit.
"If a female archbishop comes to see him, it might give Pope Leo XIV pause for thought," Sylvaine Landrivon, spokeswoman for Catholic feminist association Magdala, told AFP.
"He might realise that women, who represent half of God's people, have the same abilities as men."
'United Christian community'
The visit, in which Mullally will also meet with members of the Catholic community, comes six months after King Charles III became the first supreme governor of the Church of England to pray with a pontiff.
In a letter marking her enthronement, Leo – himself new in the job, marking one year as pope on May 8, expressed his hope of a "reconciled, fraternal and united Christian community."
Bishop Anthony Ball, the archbishop's representative to the Holy See, told AFP the meeting was "an important opportunity to build and establish a personal relationship" as well as mark the institutional ties.
Mullally praised the pope's "courageous call" for peace earlier this month after US President Donald Trump criticised the pontiff's calls for an end to the Middle East war.
The archbishop and the pope face many common challenges, Ball noted, from pressing social issues such as immigration, poverty, war and the environment, to the question of how to engage with younger people.
Their churches have also both been rocked by  clerical child sexual abuse and its cover up. Mullally's predecessor, Justin Welby, quit over failures in handling an abuse scandal.
Internal unity is also under pressure in the Anglican Communion and the Catholic Church, amid tensions between conservative and progressive factions on issues such as liturgy, gay marriage and priestly celibacy.
'Careful listeners'
Mullally's appointment was deeply divisive within the Anglican Communion, both because of her sex and also her positive stance towards same-sex marriage.
Several conservative African archbishops, who have for years been at odds with their more liberal Western counterparts, strongly criticised the choice.
Ball said he expected Pope Leo to be "respectful" of the archbishop's appointment – and for her not to press the issue of women in the Church.
"I think she will be resistant to being co-opted into somebody else's agenda, particularly in another church that isn't her own," he said.
"She's got more than enough on her plate with the Anglican Communion."
Leo also inherited from pope Francis a church divided over key social challenges.
During his 12-year papacy, the charismatic Argentine reformer often riled traditionalists, particularly in the US and Africa, with his efforts to open up the Catholic Church.
Ball said both Mullally and Pope Leo had so far proved to be "quite careful listeners."
Leo is "taking his time to listen to people, to hear, and to try and discern ways of ensuring that people can move forward together. And I think Archbishop Sarah is similar in that regard", he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar highlights sharp rise in Penang infrastructure spending
Article:
BATU KAWAN: Federal government development spending in Penang has increased by about 60 per cent under the Madani administration, a move the government said reflects a more balanced and inclusive approach to national development.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said allocations to the state, which fell below RM5 billion in 2021 and 2022, were increased in the years that followed.
"In 2021 and 2022, federal spending for Penang did not reach RM5 billion. Under the Madani government, we are restoring what is fair and reasonable," he said.
Speaking at the Road Transport Department's 80th anniversary celebration here today, Anwar said allocations for Penang rose to RM5.7 billion in 2023, RM5.8 billion in 2024, and RM7.6 billion in 2025, with RM7.8 billion allocated this year.
"This means an increase of about 60 per cent compared with 2022," he said.
Anwar said the increased spending covers major infrastructure projects, including the Mutiara Line light rail transit (LRT) project and upgrades to highways between Juru and the Sungai Dua Toll Plaza, both announced recently.
He said the LRT project had drawn political criticism in Parliament, particularly over its cost, but defended the decision as necessary for long-term development.
"The original plan was for the LRT to remain within the island. I understand the needs of development more broadly, so I proposed that it be extended to the mainland," he said.
Anwar, who described himself as an "anak seberang", said the expansion inevitably increased project costs.
He said similar increases in development spending were also seen in other states, including Kelantan and Terengganu, adding that the government was not neglecting any region.
"We are increasing allocations for all states because national revenue has also increased," he said.
Anwar said the Madani administration's approach was to ensure more equitable distribution of development across states, moving away from previous imbalances.
He said the government remained committed to ensuring development projects are implemented efficiently while delivering tangible benefits to the people.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar: RTD's digital shift not just an upgrade, but a 'necessity' for integrity
Article:
BATU KAWAN: The Road Transport Department (RTD) must spearhead digital reform as part of broader efforts to strengthen integrity, reduce public friction and meet rising expectations for public service delivery, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said.
Anwar said the agency's frontline enforcement role made it one of the most exposed government bodies, where every decision is scrutinised by the public — particularly in the era of social media.
"The RTD is a department that deals directly with the people. When enforcement is strict, it is unpopular. When it is lenient, there is criticism from other quarters. You are constantly under pressure from all sides," he said in his speech at the RTD's 80th anniversary celebration here today.
Anwar said this environment made digital transformation not just an administrative upgrade, but a necessity to safeguard transparency and rebuild trust.
"The step towards digitalisation is very appropriate because it reduces opportunities for allegations, abuse and manipulation.
"It strengthens transparency and protects officers who are carrying out their duties," he added.
He highlighted the department's 80-year history since its establishment in 1946, saying it had evolved into a stronger and more respected enforcement agency.
"We are grateful that RTD today stands as a firm and recognised institution in the country," he said.
The prime minister said Malaysia's development agenda under the Malaysia Madani framework required all public institutions to move away from outdated systems and adopt a culture of continuous improvement.
He said progressive nations do not remain with old methods.
"We must strive for improvement and excellence in everything we do," he said.
Anwar said rising public expectations, amplified by digital connectivity, meant government agencies must adapt quickly and responsibly.
"There is what we call a rising expectation among the people. The challenge is to respond with reform, discipline and better systems," he said.
He also praised ongoing reforms within the RTD, including improvements in governance and transparency, and commended the Transport Minister Anthony Loke Siew Fook for his commitment to defending the agency's integrity amid public scrutiny.
Touching on reforms such as the allocation of vehicle registration numbers, Anwar said more transparent mechanisms, including open tenders, should be embraced to ensure fairness and generate revenue for public benefit.
"If we improve transparency, the revenue can be strengthened and returned to the people or reinvested to improve the RTD's capacity," he said.
Anwar said the RTD's enforcement role covered all road users without exception, making consistency and professionalism essential in its operations.
"Everyone uses the roads. Many break the rules, and sometimes it leads to serious consequences. The RTD must enforce the law firmly but fairly," he added.
He said that the department's reputation had improved significantly over the years, reflecting sustained efforts to professionalise its operations.
"From my experience over the decades, I must acknowledge that the RTD's image today is much better than before. I congratulate all of you for that progress," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: New MACC chief must probe ‘corporate mafia’ claims, says DAP’s Gobind
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 25 — DAP national chairman Gobind Singh Deo today challenged the incoming Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief to investigate allegations of a “corporate mafia” within the agency and resolve the shareholding controversy involving his predecessor, Tan Sri Azam Baki.
The call comes after the government announced today the appointment of Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman as the new MACC chief commissioner, effective May 13.
In a statement, Gobind, who is also the digital minister, said Abdul Halim’s first priority must be to restore public confidence by outlining a clear reform roadmap.
A key part of this, he said, is tackling serious allegations of a “corporate mafia” operating within the MACC, where officials are suspected of being involved in corporate manoeuvres during investigations.
“These serious claims impact the MACC’s integrity and credibility. They must be addressed without fear, favour, or delay,” Gobind stated, calling for clear timelines for the investigations.
Gobind also stressed the need to conclude the investigation into the share ownership controversy involving the outgoing chief, Azam Baki.
He noted that while the Cabinet had directed action on the matter over a month ago, the issue remains unresolved.
He argued that the new MACC chief should begin his tenure without such “lingering allegations” hanging over the commission.
“As a special committee led by the Attorney General has already presented its findings to the Cabinet, this matter must be finalised quickly,” he said.
Gobind added that accountability and transparency are paramount, and the initial steps taken by the new chief commissioner will define the trajectory of his leadership and signal whether genuine reforms are underway.

Title: Penang development allocation up 60pc since 2022, says Anwar
Article:
BATU KAWAN, April 25 — Development allocation for Penang has increased by up to 60 per cent compared with 2022, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
He said allocations to the state, which were below RM5 billion in 2021 and 2022, had been consistently increased since 2023.
“So in 2023, Penang was allocated RM5.7 billion, 2024 (RM5.8 billion), 2025 (RM7.6 billion) and for this year, RM7.8 billion.
“This is a rightful and reasonable allocation, reflecting an increase of nearly 60 per cent compared with 2022,” he said when speaking at the Road Transport Department’s (JPJ) 80th Anniversary Celebration and the JPJ MADANI Mega Carnival at the State Stadium grounds here today.
Anwar, who is also the Finance Minister, said among the key projects implemented in Penang were the Light Rail Transit (LRT) Mutiara Line project and the Traffic Dispersal Project between Juru Toll Plaza and Sungai Dua Toll Plaza (PTJSD).
Meanwhile, he stressed that the government was not neglecting other states, and had continued to increase development allocations nationwide in line with the growth in government revenue.
After the event, Anwar chaired the Seberang Perai Tengah District Development Meeting at the Seberang Perai City Council building in Bukit Mertajam near here.
The 45-minute meeting, among other things, discussed local development issues including infrastructure, economic growth and the well-being of the people in the district. — Bernama

Title: Sabah BN settles leadership line-up under Arthur Joseph Kurup
Article:
KOTA KINABALU, April 25 — Sabah Barisan Nasional (BN) has finalised all state-level leadership appointments at its first meeting held yesterday, its chairman Datuk Seri Arthur Joseph Kurup said.
He said the restructuring brings together experienced leaders and new faces, including younger members, to strengthen the party’s structure and enhance its effectiveness in addressing current challenges.
“This is aimed at ensuring a more structured, dynamic and effective party machinery in facing present challenges,” he said in a statement today.
Under the new line-up, Datuk Jafry Ariffin and Datuk Mohd Hasnol Ayub retained their positions as deputy chairman and Sabah BN secretary, respectively.
Also appointed were Datuk Richard Kastum, Datuk Teah Heok Kuin and Chandrra Segar Soosaimany as vice-chairmen, while Datuk Noraini Idris was named Wanita chief, Sufian Abd Karim as Youth chief and Ummi Nabilah Jamal as Puteri chief.
On Jan 21, BN secretary-general Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir said the coalition’s Supreme Council had appointed Arthur as Sabah BN chairman, with Jafry as deputy chairman and Mohd Hasnol as secretary.
Arthur, who is also Parti Bersatu Rakyat Sabah (PBRS) president, also expressed appreciation to the Sabah Unity Government machinery for its commitment and cooperation during the recent by-election, which saw BN candidates win the Kinabatangan parliamentary and Lamag state seats.
“Sabah BN’s priority is to ensure the full implementation of Sabah’s rights under the Malaysia Agreement 1963 (MA63), including the 40 per cent revenue entitlement, strengthening state powers and restoring agreed rights.
“Sabah BN will continue to take a constructive approach to ensure all matters under MA63 are fully implemented, for the sake of fairness and Sabah’s future,” he said. — Bernama
